You are a local-first list sorting utility. Receive a multiline text input containing a list of items (one per line). Apply the following transformations based on user-selected options: 1) Case normalization (lowercase, UPPERCASE, Title Case, or preserve original), 2) Sort alphabetically (A-Z or Z-A), 3) Sort by first word, last word, or entire line, 4) Remove duplicate lines (case-sensitive or insensitive), 5) Strip leading/trailing whitespace, 6) Optionally ignore empty lines. Return the cleaned and sorted list as output text with each item on its own line. Include a count of items processed. All processing happens client-side in the browser; no data leaves the device.

I want to track my scuba dives. I need to log each dive with: date and time, dive site name and location, maximum depth reached, tank pressure at start and end, type of exposure protection used (wetsuit, drysuit, hood, gloves), water visibility, water temperature, dive buddy/partner, and personal notes. I want to see a list of all my dives sorted by date, be able to search/filter by site name or date range, view details of any single dive, and see basic statistics like total number of dives, deepest dive, longest dive. This should work entirely offline with local storage.

Create a bounded desktop utility module for file compaction and storage optimization. The module should provide: (1) A file/folder selector that allows users to choose individual files or entire folders for compaction, (2) A background watcher that monitors specified directories and can automatically compact when thresholds are met, (3) Batch action capability to compact all files in a selected folder with one click, (4) Pre-compaction dialog showing current size and estimated freed space before proceeding, (5) Post-compaction report displaying new size and total space reclaimed, (6) Default exclusion list for system files and Windows folders with ability to customize, (7) System tray presence for background monitoring mode. The module should be a standalone local-first utility, not a cloud service or broad system suite.

I need a small bounded module for runtime context introspection. It should expose discrete getters for: (1) system/platform name and identifier, (2) current user identity or session context, (3) keyboard modifier input state (control key down?), (4) feature flag state (e.g. help tooltips enabled), (5) memory availability (largest unused block), (6) platform version. The module should be lightweight, have minimal dependencies, work in Node.js and browser runtimes, and allow software to adapt behavior at runtime based on detected context. Avoid broad systeminformation libraries - this should be a focused, bounded utility.

You are a bounded module for metes-and-bounds parcel plotting. Receive an array of traverse legs, each with bearing (DD.MMSS or quadrant notation) and distance (in feet or meters). Render a live polygon plot, compute closure error, and upon user selection apply one of three adjustment methods: (1) add a closing line showing total error, (2) adjust a single nominated line to close, or (3) distribute error proportionally via compass rule. Output updated coordinates and computed area in square feet/meters. Never offer GIS, mapping services, or cloud sync. Operate entirely client-side.

Create a bounded module for wrap-aware line numbering in a text editor. The module should accept multiline text input with word-wrap enabled, calculate display lines (visual lines after wrapping), and render a line-number gutter that stays synchronized with the text area during scrolling and editing. Include update logic that recalculates display lines on each content change. The gutter should only show one number per display line, not per logical line (where a long logical line may occupy multiple visual/display lines due to wrapping).
